ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, THEODORE T. CORDERY'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. RICH-TEX, INC. WAS PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE MOTION AND THERE IS NO OPPOSITION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST THAT THE COURT STRIKE RICH-TEX, INC.'S ANSWER AND ENTER DEFAULT AGAINST IT IS DENIED AT THIS TIME, HOWEVER PLAINTIFF IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM SEEKING AVAILABLE RELIEF BROUGHT IN PROPER FORM AND PROCEDURE. ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: THEODORE T. CORDERY'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Theodore T. Cordery's motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant Rich-Tex, Inc. is granted. Rich-Tex, Inc. was properly served with the motion and there is no opposition. Plaintiff's request that the Court strike Rich-Tex, Inc.'s answer and enter default against it is denied at this time, however plaintiff is not precluded from seeking available relief brought in proper form and procedure. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/CML)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
